candidate.title: Complete DID method resolution algorithm per W3C DID Core §7
candidate.description: spec/did-method-bolyra.md defines the DID syntax (did:bolyra:<commitment>) but lacks a conformant resolution algorithm. Implement the resolve(did, resolutionOptions) → {didDocument, didResolutionMetadata, didDocumentMetadata} interface per W3C DID Core §7.1. The resolver must query IdentityRegistry for enrollment status, populate the verificationMethod array with the BabyJubjub public key (type JsonWebKey2020 with crv=Baby-Jubjub), and set didDocumentMetadata.deactivated=true for revoked identities. Without this, did:bolyra cannot pass the W3C DID Test Suite.

candidate.title: Standardize proof envelope wire format with CBOR content type negotiation
candidate.description: The SDK currently passes proof objects as ad-hoc JSON with no versioning, no content type, and no canonical serialization. Define a CBOR-based proof envelope (tag 55799 self-describing CBOR) with fields: version, circuit_id, proving_system (groth16|plonk), public_signals[], proof_bytes, and an optional delegation_chain[]. Register application/bolyra-proof+cbor as the canonical media type. This is prerequisite for any HTTP-based interop — without it, every integration reinvents parsing.

candidate.title: Define off-chain session token format for verified handshakes
candidate.description: After a successful on-chain handshake verification, there is no standardized bearer token format for the resulting session. Define a compact JWT-like session token (JWS with EdDSA over BabyJubjub) that encodes the verified nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, and expiry. This enables off-chain relying parties to accept Bolyra auth results without querying the chain for every API call. Include IANA media type registration for application/bolyra-session+jwt and a COSE equivalent for constrained environments.

candidate.title: Publish deterministic conformance test vectors in JSON Schema
candidate.description: Create a spec/test-vectors/ directory with 30+ JSON files covering: valid handshake (happy path), expired agent credential, revoked human identity, stale Merkle root, scope subset violation, cumulative bit encoding violation, delegation chain depths 1/2/3, and max-hop rejection. Each vector includes input witness, expected public signals, and expected accept/reject outcome. Validate against a JSON Schema so alternative implementations can run the suite mechanically. This is the single biggest interop enabler — no third party can implement Bolyra without reproducible test cases.

candidate.title: Off-chain JWT session token from verified handshake
candidate.description: After a successful on-chain handshake verification, emit a signed JWT containing the nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, and expiry so that LangChain/CrewAI tool chains can pass a lightweight bearer token on subsequent calls instead of re-proving. The SDK should expose `handshakeToSessionToken(proof, signingKey) → JWT` and `verifySessionToken(jwt, pubKey) → {nullifier, scope, expiry}`. This eliminates the biggest integration friction: frameworks expect sub-100ms auth checks, not 2-second proof generation on every tool invocation.

candidate.title: Standard proof envelope with MIME content type for HTTP transport
candidate.description: Define an `application/bolyra-proof+json` content type and a canonical JSON envelope: `{version, circuit, publicSignals[], proof{pi_a, pi_b, pi_c}, sessionToken?, delegationChain[]}`. LangChain's `APIChain` and AutoGen's `ConversableAgent` both pass structured payloads over HTTP — without a standard envelope, every integration reinvents serialization. Add `ProofEnvelope.serialize()` / `ProofEnvelope.parse()` to both TS and Python SDKs, with round-trip test vectors in `spec/test-vectors/`.

candidate.title: Off-chain session token with on-chain checkpoint batching
candidate.description: After a successful handshake, emit a signed session token (JWT-like envelope containing humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, expiry) that downstream tool calls can verify locally without hitting the chain. Batch session roots on-chain every N minutes via a Merkle accumulator. This is the unlock for agentic commerce at scale — no agent-to-agent interaction can tolerate per-call on-chain verification latency or gas. Implement in the TS SDK as `createSessionToken()` and `verifySessionToken()`, with the batching contract as a new `SessionCheckpoint.sol`.

candidate.title: Prove cumulative bit encoding constraints are necessary and sufficient
candidate.description: The Delegation circuit enforces cumulative bit invariants (bit 4 implies bits 2+3, bit 3 implies bit 2) on the delegatee scope. Formally verify two properties: (1) SOUNDNESS — no witness satisfying the circuit constraints can violate the implication rules, and (2) COMPLETENESS — every valid cumulative encoding is accepted. Write a standalone test harness that exhaustively checks all 2^8 permission byte values against the circuit constraints and confirms exact agreement with the SDK's validateCumulativeBitEncoding(). This also catches any off-by-one in bit indexing between Circom (LSB-first Num2Bits) and the Solidity/TS layer.
